You can just build things

This is the story how @AlexSmirnov built the safest bridge in crypto

> Left his PhD to go full-time crypto in 2016

> At 24, founded a blockchain dev shop that built & patented a crypto custody solution for financial institutions

> 2020: running cross-chain arb across MEV-heavy chains

> CEX halted his withdrawals

> Decided to build non-custodial cross-chain liquidity rails

> Built deBridge MVP at first hackathon & won

> 2021: received a $20K Solana grant & pushed early integration

> Raised $5.5M seed

> Built the first 0-TVL, intent-based bridge

> Expanded to 25+ chains

> 2026: $20B+ bridged, 0 hacks / security incidents

> 4.5% of DBR supply accumulated by DAO with $10M protocol earnings

You can just build things

Wealth tax on unrealized capital gains is not a wealth tax, it’s a capital tax.  

What’s the difference?

A farmer owns the farm.  The wealth is what the lands produces.  The crops, the food.  

The capital is the title to the farm.  The piece of paper representing stock owning the farm. The farmer can borrow against it, sell it in whole or in part. 

How much the farmer can sell the property is based on how much food the farmer produces.  

What the @SenSanders tax is suggesting is that the farmer that is the most successful at delivering the most food to the most people should own the least of his farm.  

Every year that farmer should give up 5% ownership of the farm.

introducing the next Backpack token utility, we'll be building out this year. IPOs, on chain, directly on Backpack.  

Normally when companies go public, the founders and executives go on a roadshow, sharing their story and garnering interest from Wall Street institutional buyers purchasing shares before they go public. For most, you normally have to wait for the stock exchanges to go live before getting access to these shares through your brokerage.  

What we're doing: working with Superstate for Backpack to become a stop on the roadshow, so that our users can get access to these IPOs before trading goes live on national stock exchanges.   

The catch: Our ability to do this successfully is purely a function of the Backpack community.

The more active and valuable our users, the more viable Backpack is a venue for capital formation. As a result, we've put together this waitlist to collect interest from our users today on this product. The early users that signup will get early access to the first IPO.  

The more people that sign up for this, the more engaged and active our community is here, the better access we will get to companies when they go public.  

This will be the first test of the community. This is an enormously valuable opportunity, and we need your help signing up for this so that we can go out and get the best IPOs for everyone here that we can.  

The ask: add yourself to the waitlist now.   

We have a lot more coming on this front, and this is a perfect example of why we did not build a DEX. This is only something a compliant product can do. 

Go big or go home.

On Monday, I received a DM from @depitchsofian from @depitch_academy.

He asked me to share materials about @NOMADZxyz, as he wants to include it as an example of a successful startup in his pitch masterclass during the @solanamobile and @RadiantsDAO Monolith Hackathon.

DePitch helps founders master the art of pitching their startups.

I first met Sofian and @L0STE_ after Nomadz won Week’s Top Project on @legendsdotfun. As a founder, I was invited to have my pitch roasted live on @superteam's  X space.

I remember getting some truly valuable insights on how to improve my pitch. 

Here are the key takeaways:

1. Show real traction: registered users, revenue, and how you achieved those numbers.
2. Keep one clear message per roadmap milestone (Q1, Q2, Q3, etc.).
3. Highlight one memorable achievement for each team member you introduce.
4. Always end with a strong CTA that leads to the next step (demo, call, signup).
5. Prepare three versions of your pitch: 5-second, 30-second, and 5-minute formats.

It feels surreal that such a short time has passed since that pitch, and today my project is being highlighted among hackathon winners as an example for founders building in the space.

Have you ever had your pitch roasted before?

most things i’ve really liked about this new ui update on @meteora or Dynamic Terminal is you can be more profitable with this new ui.

but how? this new ui prioritizing chart of the pair that you deposited to.

it means :

- you can absolutely monitoring the chart while monitoring your position
- you get the realtime motion of the price action
- you can decide quickly when to exit or enter without checking the chart on screneer

for a degen play this is absolutely helpfull because you doesn’t need to leave the app and gib you the all-in-one access on one page.

pool details also very details about advanced metrics really help you to decide is this pool printing good or not. for me, for a quick play i’ll look into this and check

- open positions - to check how much positions opened help you to give confidence
- fees/active tvl - above 7.5% is really good
- volatility - to decide how’s the range

btw, thanks for implementing people feedbacks when this ui roll out on meteora edge page.

We built this because LPs don’t have time to mess around. 

After years in the trenches with the LP army, we rebuilt DLMM around what actually matters: speed, access to the data you need at your fingertips, and a setup you can customize to fit your needs. 

Our long-term goal is to make Meteora the one-stop shop for everything liquidity - starting with making it the absolute best place to LP.

We kept refining it with your feedback to deliver the experience LPs actually need.

And today… we’re excited to bring the Dynamic Terminal to life.

Stay tuned. We’re shipping more powerful new features very soon.

Try it now at https://t.co/kZxjRSuiGU

🚨Solflare Launches Wallet-Native Privacy Layer on Solana 
@solflare introduces PAL (Privacy Aggregator Layer) - a built-in privacy routing system directly inside the wallet 👇

  ▶️Private Send : Optional privacy for transfers, reducing visible on-chain links between sender and receiver.
  ▶️Seamless Routing : Transactions are automatically routed through integrated privacy partners.
  ▶️Self-Custodial : Users stay in full control of their funds.
  ▶️Transparent Fees : Costs shown upfront before confirming.

🚨 Airdrop alert (limited time)

Win $20K for beating this AI and qualify for an airdrop on top

Stormrae is building a human participation layer for AI security

Basically they pay people to stress-test AI models through games

Their first challenge is "King Arthur:"

Users buy credits → send prompts trying to convince the AI to “pull the sword” (break its rules)

The first person to succeed wins 50% of the pool (currently $20K)

Then it resets
The pool keeps growing

While trying to break the AI you can:

- Earn Shards (think $STORM airdrop)
- Earn Echoes (10% of credit purchases from your referrals)

You’ve got 7 days

RT @0xFA2: Fuck it. We continue building.

In February, my friend @ftmska sent me a post from @garrytan about AI agents adoption.

Garry is a CEO at @ycombinator, one of the largest accelerators for founders, that has invested in top companies like Airbnb, Stripe, Coinbase, and Dropbox.

The main insight from the post and its research was that 50% of verticals are not yet using AI agents.

Software engineering accounts for nearly half of all AI agent usage. The remaining half is spread across 16 different verticals, and none of them exceeds 9%.

What I took from this:

1. There are many verticals with significant opportunities for founders right now.
2. The reality where agents replace 5 or even 10 employees is already here.
3. Companies that do not integrate AI automation risk losing their market position.

Since reading that post, at @NOMADZxyz we have launched V2 of our AI Search, where an agent processes user requests and returns matching options.

According to the stats, travel and logistics represent roughly 1% of AI agent usage. For me, that is a clear gap and a strong growth opportunity.

Have you already integrated agents into your business or daily workflow?

🚨 Solana Launches Agent Registry - Powering the Agent Economy On-Chain

@solana has introduced Agent Registry, a native on-chain system that allows AI agents to register verifiable identities, build tamper-proof track records, and receive independent third-party attestations.

Built as Solana’s implementation of the ERC-8004 “Trustless Agents” standard (developed by @Quantu_AI), the registry includes:
▫️ Verified Profiles - on-chain identity, capabilities, and ownership
▫️Running Track Records - transparent, immutable performance history
▫️Independent Proofs - third-party verification of completed tasks

The recent announcement from X regarding paid partnerships applies broadly across crypto, regardless of how many followers you have.

Given this update, educational content from the LP Army Content Machine about how to use Meteora may potentially fall under the paid promotion guidelines.

With that said, LP Army content on product usage is not controlled by @MeteoraAG. This includes (but is not limited to):

- PnL posts
- Breakdowns of your personal strategies
- Review of team news or product updates
- Personal memes & entertainment

Each win and strategy shared is an authentic experience from community members who genuinely want to educate others and share tools that give them an edge.

This also extends beyond the Content Machine. LP Army often hosts competitions, and if you are participating in those with potential monetary rewards, you may want to review whether disclosure is appropriate under X’s updated policy.

If you are simply sharing your own PnL card or personal strategies as an LP, then there is no partnership or ad to disclose.

However, when in doubt, review the policy and disclose where necessary.

We’ll work together to build transparency within the LP Army.

I have never in my life seen a major city where over 90% of the population are expats and openly agree their government genuinely wants the best for them.

That city is Dubai.

After living in 7 countries and being able to live anywhere in the world, I chose to live in Dubai. That wasn’t a coincidence.

Most people in Dubai were NOT born and raised here. They CHOOSE to be here and they still support the UAE as if it were their own country.

No one is forced to say good things about the government. That narrative is all social media bullshit that many people consume.

I’m Argentinian. I never praised my own government. I lived in Spain and I never praised their government. Loving a country and defending its leadership are two different things.

When I’m at a random house with friends in Dubai, no cameras, no agenda, everyone says the same thing. People are truly grateful. They all speak positively.

Residents thanked the government after 96% of missiles and drones were intercepted. Trust increased, not decreased.

Over 20,000 stranded tourists received free hotels and meals financed by Abu Dhabi. That’s not social media bs. That’s real action and empathy for people. EU countries or the US would never do that for their own people, do you think they’d do it for tourists?

I’ve lived in enough places to know what a comfortable life looks like. I never told anyone to move to the other countries I lived in.

Despite every negative experience I’ve had abroad, I have never hated an entire country. The hate the UAE or Dubai receives is baseless and envy-driven.

I don’t trust people who blindly hate a country they’ve never lived in.

You don’t have to agree with me, but at least experience a place before assuming you understand it because you went there once on holiday.

I have love for ALL nations. I would rather die than hate a whole country or city.
